fields.name: pre_visit_planning
fields.description:
These help frame the objectives and references to read before and carry during the visit.

Examples include:
• Visit Terms of Reference (ToR)
• Monitoring checklist or tool (aligned with program indicators)
• Workplan / Itinerary
• Maps / Site lists / Location details
• Previous monitoring reports for the same location or workplan activity/output
• Previous Partner reports for triangulation

fields.name: administrative_compliance
fields.description:
To verify adherence to donor, organizational, and regulatory requirements.

Examples include:
• Partner agreements / MoUs / Previous Programme Document/Simplified Programme Document (PD/SPD) for the same partner
• Procurement documents (POs, invoices, delivery notes)
• Asset or inventory registers
• Compliance checklists (if applicable)

fields.name: field_data_collection
fields.description:
Tools used on-site to record findings in addition to the FM checklist.

Examples include:
• Interview or FGD guides used
• Enumerator/monitoring forms (digital or paper) including additional checklists used
• Attendance sheets (for activities or training sessions)
• Quality assurance (QA/QC) checklists beyond FMM checklist

fields.name: evidence_verification
fields.description:
Proof that activities took place as reported. To assess progress and alignment with program goals.

Examples include:
• Photographs with metadata (e.g., date, GPS)
• GPS coordinates / maps
• Signed beneficiary confirmations (when appropriate)
• Delivery records / Distribution lists
• Receipts, vouchers, or tokens
• Infrastructure assessment sheets
• Before/after photos (for construction or rehabilitation work)
• Activity reports from partners
• Training materials used in the field
• Curricula or technical guidelines
• Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) indicator tables
• Gender, protection, or safeguarding documentation

fields.name: risk_safeguarding_protection
fields.description:
Especially important for donor‑funded or protection‑sensitive programs.

Examples include:
• Incident reporting forms (not capturing beneficiary identity but trigger document)
• Safeguarding compliance checklists
• Protection mainstreaming tool
• Risk assessment forms
• PSEA (Protection from Sexual Exploitation and Abuse) guidance

fields.name: post_visit_documentation
fields.description:
To close the loop and document follow‑up.

Examples include:
• Field monitoring report (if anything beyond what the FMM report captures)
• Action point tracker (with responsibility + deadline)
• Partner feedback form
• Follow‑up verification plan
• Corrective action documentation
